req.title: Manager deletes document
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to delete a document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated manager can delete a document via the UI, with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and correct HTTP responses. I designed: (1) a positive EP test confirming successful deletion, audit trail, and 200/201 response for a valid manager session; (2) a NEG test confirming 403 when a non-manager role (employee) attempts deletion; (3) a BVA test targeting the boundary of the document ID field (minimum valid ID = 1 vs. ID = 0 which is invalid); (4) a STC test verifying the full state transition from 'document exists' through 'deletion in progress' to 'document deleted'; (5) a DT test covering the decision matrix of authentication x role x document existence to confirm 401 when unauthenticated.

tests.name: test_manager_deletes_document_successfully
preconditions: A document with known ID exists in the system
actions: Submit the delete form with all mandatory fields populated
expected: Response status is 200 or 201
Response body contains the deleted entity ID
Document no longer appears in the document list
An audit record is written for the deletion event
trace: R-WEB-031: audit record written on success
R-WEB-031: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_deletes_document_successfully(manager_page, create_document, audit_log_client):
    doc_id = create_document(title="EP Test Doc", owner="manager")
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/documents")
    page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{doc_id}'] [data-action='delete']").click()
    page.locator("[data-testid='confirm-delete-btn']").click()
    response = page.wait_for_response(lambda r: "/documents" in r.url and r.request.method in ("DELETE", "POST"))
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"
    body = response.json()
    assert str(doc_id) in str(body.get("id", body.get("entity_id", "")))
    expect(page.locator(f"[data-doc-id='{doc_id}']")).not_to_be_visible()
    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=doc_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written"

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_forbidden_from_deleting_document
preconditions: User is authenticated as employee (non-manager role)
A document with known ID exists in the system
actions: Attempt to POST/DELETE to /documents endpoint for an existing document ID
expected: Response status is 403
Document is NOT deleted
No audit record written for deletion
Error message indicates insufficient permissions
trace: R-WEB-031: error pathway 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_manager_role_forbidden_from_deleting_document(employee_api_client, create_document, audit_log_client):
    doc_id = create_document(title="NEG Forbidden Doc", owner="manager")
    response = employee_api_client.delete(f"/documents/{doc_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "forbidden" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "permission" in body.get("message", "").lower()
    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=doc_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must not be written on forbidden attempt"
    existing = employee_api_client.get(f"/documents/{doc_id}")
    assert existing.status_code != 404, "Document must still exist after forbidden attempt"

tests.name: test_delete_document_with_boundary_minimum_valid_id
preconditions: Document with ID equal to minimum valid integer (1) exists
Document with ID equal to 0 does not exist (invalid boundary)
actions: Attempt to delete document with ID=0 (below minimum boundary)
Attempt to delete document with ID=1 (minimum valid boundary)
Verify 200 or 201 response and audit record
expected: Delete request with ID=0 returns 400 validation error
Delete request with ID=1 returns 200 or 201
Audit record written only for ID=1 deletion
Document with ID=1 is removed from the system
trace: R-WEB-031: boundary value of document ID field
R-WEB-031: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_delete_document_with_boundary_minimum_valid_id(manager_api_client, ensure_document_id_1_exists, audit_log_client):
    resp_invalid = manager_api_client.delete("/documents/0")
    assert resp_invalid.status_code == 400, f"Expected 400 for ID=0, got {resp_invalid.status_code}"
    error_body = resp_invalid.json()
    assert "error" in error_body or "message" in error_body
    audit_zero = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=0, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_zero) == 0, "No audit for invalid ID=0"
    ensure_document_id_1_exists()
    resp_valid = manager_api_client.delete("/documents/1")
    assert resp_valid.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 for ID=1, got {resp_valid.status_code}"
    body = resp_valid.json()
    assert str(body.get("id", body.get("entity_id", ""))) == "1"
    audit_one = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=1, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_one) >= 1, "Audit record must exist for valid deletion"

tests.name: test_document_state_transition_exists_to_deleted
preconditions: Document is in state ACTIVE (exists and retrievable)
actions: Verify document is in ACTIVE state via GET /documents/{id}
Navigate to /documents and initiate delete flow
Confirm deletion; observe intermediate PENDING_DELETE state if applicable
Verify final state is DELETED via GET /documents/{id} returning 404 or status=DELETED
expected: Initial GET returns 200 and state ACTIVE
Delete operation returns 200 or 201
Final GET returns 404 or document status equals DELETED
Audit record reflects state transition to DELETED
trace: R-WEB-031: state transition from document exists to document deleted
R-WEB-031: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_document_state_transition_exists_to_deleted(manager_api_client, create_document, audit_log_client):
    doc_id = create_document(title="STC Transition Doc", owner="manager")
    pre_resp = manager_api_client.get(f"/documents/{doc_id}")
    assert pre_resp.status_code == 200
    pre_body = pre_resp.json()
    assert pre_body.get("status", "ACTIVE").upper() in ("ACTIVE", "PUBLISHED", "DRAFT")
    del_resp = manager_api_client.delete(f"/documents/{doc_id}")
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {del_resp.status_code}"
    post_resp = manager_api_client.get(f"/documents/{doc_id}")
    if post_resp.status_code == 200:
        assert post_resp.json().get("status", "").upper() == "DELETED"
    else:
        assert post_resp.status_code == 404
    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=doc_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1
    assert audit_entries[0].get("new_state", "").upper() in ("DELETED", "REMOVED")

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_document
preconditions: No active session / no auth token
A document with known ID exists
actions: Send DELETE request to /documents/{id} with no authentication header
expected: Response status is 401
Response body contains authentication error message
Document is NOT deleted
No audit record written
trace: DT row: unauthenticated x any role x document exists => 401
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_delete_document(anon_api_client, create_document, manager_api_client, audit_log_client):
    doc_id = create_document(title="DT Unauth Doc", owner="manager")
    response = anon_api_client.delete(f"/documents/{doc_id}")
    assert response.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {response.status_code}"
    body = response.json()
    assert "unauthorized" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "authentication" in body.get("message", "").lower()
    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=doc_id, action="DELETE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "No audit record should be written for unauthenticated attempt"
    still_exists = manager_api_client.get(f"/documents/{doc_id}")
    assert still_exists.status_code == 200, "Document must still exist after unauthenticated attempt"
